generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance HeatbedActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Resistive heating element embedded in styrofoam box with PWM control for adjustable thermal energy output. Operates within thermal insulation constraints and includes over-temperature detection for safety."
]

instance FanActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Low-power axial fan with variable-speed control for uniform air distribution within styrofoam enclosure. Operational limits governed by airflow resistance from styrofoam structure."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance InternalTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 high-precision 1-Wire digital thermometer positioned within styrofoam box to measure internal temperature with ±0.5°C accuracy over -55°C to +125°C range."
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance ExternalTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 high-precision 1-Wire digital thermometer deployed externally to monitor ambient conditions for environmental compensation."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ExternalTemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance BoundaryTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 high-precision 1-Wire digital thermometer positioned near heatbed to capture thermal gradients within the styrofoam enclosure."
    DTDFVocab:producedData BoundaryTemperatureDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance InternalTemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor
]

instance ExternalTemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ExternalTemperatureSensor
]

instance BoundaryTemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom BoundaryTemperatureSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance GeometricModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "CAD-based geometric representation capturing physical dimensions, shapes, and structural properties of styrofoam enclosure and internal components."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CalibrationAlgorithm
]

instance BehavioralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Dynamic simulation model using differential equations to analyze thermal distribution, airflow patterns, and heatbed-fan interactions within the enclosure."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CalibrationAlgorithm
]

instance EnvironmentalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Analytical model predicting system behavior under varying environmental conditions including ambient temperature effects and thermal gradients."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CalibrationAlgorithm
]

instance HeatDistributionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Analytical model predicting heat transfer patterns within the styrofoam enclosure accounting for thermal gradients and insulation properties."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CalibrationAlgorithm
]

instance AirflowModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Dynamic simulation model of airflow patterns generated by the fan within the constrained styrofoam enclosure."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CalibrationAlgorithm
]

instance InternalTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time temperature measurements from internal sensor stream, including ±0.5°C accuracy readings transmitted via 1-Wire protocol."
    DTDFVocab:fromData InternalTemperatureDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CalibrationAlgorithm
]

instance ExternalTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Ambient temperature measurements for environmental compensation, used to adjust calibration parameters and model predictions."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ExternalTemperatureDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CalibrationAlgorithm
]

instance BoundaryTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Thermal gradient data from boundary sensor used to validate heat distribution models and detect thermal anomalies."
    DTDFVocab:fromData BoundaryTemperatureDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CalibrationAlgorithm
]

// C11: Enablers
instance RabbitMQBroker : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Message broker implementing RabbitMQ protocol for asynchronous data exchange between sensors, calibration algorithms, and monitoring services."
]

instance PythonProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Python-based processing pipeline utilizing SciPy for numerical operations in model calibration, parameter estimation, and data preprocessing."
    DTDFVocab:enables CalibrationAlgorithm
]

instance RaspberryPiHardware : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Embedded computing unit hosting data acquisition, processing, and communication interfaces for the Physical Twin system."
]

instance CalibrationAlgorithm : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Non-linear least squares solver implemented in Python for refining model parameters using real-time sensor data to align DT predictions with observed PT behavior."
]

// C6: Services
instance DataAcquisitionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time data collection service aggregating sensor streams from Physical Twin components via 1-Wire protocol and RabbitMQ broker."
    DTDFVocab:provides TemperatureDataInsight, EnvironmentalDataInsight
]

instance CalibrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Model refinement service applying calibration algorithms to update DT parameters with real-world sensor data for improved accuracy."
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelAccuracyInsight, ParameterOptimizationAction
]

instance MonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time and historical performance monitoring service detecting violations against safety and optimality thresholds using fault detectors."
    DTDFVocab:provides FaultDetectionInsight, PerformanceAnalysisInsight
]

instance SimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Virtual replication service running dynamic simulations to predict PT behavior under current and hypothetical operating conditions."
    DTDFVocab:provides PredictiveAnalysisInsight, ScenarioTestingAction
]

instance OptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Decision-support service using optimizers to improve system performance through adaptive control and parameter tuning."
    DTDFVocab:provides PerformanceOptimizationInsight, ControlAdjustmentAction
]

instance ControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Autonomous intervention service detecting violations and triggering corrective actions through algorithmic or human-assisted adjustments."
    DTDFVocab:provides FaultDetectionInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance TemperatureDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time internal temperature measurements with ±0.5°C accuracy, used for thermal regulation and anomaly detection."
]

instance EnvironmentalDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Ambient and boundary temperature data for environmental compensation and model validation."
]

instance FaultDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identification of anomalies or deviations from safe/optimal operating conditions based on real-time sensor data."
]

instance PerformanceAnalysisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Comprehensive assessment of system performance against safety and optimality thresholds using historical and real-time data."
]

instance ModelAccuracyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Quantitative measure of DT model alignment with Physical Twin behavior through calibration validation metrics."
]

instance PredictiveAnalysisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Forecasted system behavior under current or modified operating conditions derived from simulation models."
]

instance PerformanceOptimizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Recommendations for system performance improvements based on optimization algorithms and real-time data."
]

instance ParameterOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated adjustment of model parameters to improve calibration accuracy using non-linear least squares methods."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ControlAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Dynamic adjustment of heatbed/fan operation to maintain target temperature profiles based on real-time monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance CorrectiveAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated or human-assisted intervention to correct detected violations or suboptimal conditions in the Physical Twin."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ScenarioTestingAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Execution of virtual experiments to test hypothetical changes before physical implementation."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C18: Environment
instance ThermalIncubatorEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Operational environment for the Physical Twin system including styrofoam enclosure, heatbed, fan, and embedded computing components."
    base:contains StyrofoamEnclosure, HeatbedActuator, FanActuator, RaspberryPiHardware
]

instance StyrofoamEnclosure : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Thermal insulation box housing the Physical Twin components with constrained thermal conductivity affecting heat distribution."
    base:isContainedIn ThermalIncubatorEnvironment
]

// Relationships for Enablers
instance RabbitMQBroker : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    DTDFVocab:enables DataAcquisitionService, CalibrationService
]

instance RaspberryPiHardware : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    DTDFVocab:enables DataAcquisitionService, ControlService
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The **virtual-to-physical interaction** in this Digital Twin system is described through **control flows** that originate from the Digital Twin’s **algorithms** and **monitors**, which validate system performance and safety. The document highlights that when **violations** (e.g., operational deviations) are detected, **humans or other algorithms** may initiate changes to the **Physical Twin (PT)**. While the **command types** are not explicitly listed, the context implies **adjustments to system parameters** (e.g., fan speed, heatbed temperature) to restore optimal operation. **Control message formats** are not specified, but the interaction suggests **structured commands** (e.g., JSON or binary) to trigger actions. **Validation and authentication mechanisms** are not detailed, though the system’s reliance on **calibration algorithms** implies some form of **parameter verification** before command execution. **Feedback systems** are implied through the **monitoring** component, which provides real-time performance data to inform corrective actions. **Conflict resolution** is not explicitly addressed, but the document suggests a **human-in-the-loop** or **algorithm-driven** approach to resolve discrepancies. **Command execution confirmation** is inferred through the **monitoring** phase, where system performance is reassessed post-intervention."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system operates on a time scale defined by asynchronous, time-series data collection from the Physical Twin (PT) via sensors, where data is gathered *over time* rather than in real-time or with strict synchronization constraints. The system employs calibration algorithms to iteratively update models of the Cyber-Physical System (CPS) and its environment, but the exact synchronization frequency or latency requirements are not specified. The described workflow includes a monitoring loop where detected violations (e.g., safety or optimality deviations) trigger human or algorithmic intervention, implying a non-immediate feedback mechanism. No explicit mention of low-latency, near-real-time, or immediate twinning synchronization is provided in the documents."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system follows a modular architecture pattern where **data** (from PT sensors), **models** (of CPS and environment), and **algorithms** (e.g., calibration, fault detection, state estimation, optimization) are integrated into a cohesive framework. Resource management is implicit, relying on the calibration algorithms to update models with the best parameter estimates, ensuring consistency between the PT and DT. System-wide coordination is achieved through a feedback loop: monitors validate performance against safety/optimality criteria, and violations trigger human or algorithmic interventions (e.g., parameter adjustments or control actions). The orchestration emphasizes real-time adaptability, where models and algorithms dynamically interact to maintain alignment with the PT’s operational state."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
The lifecycle of a Digital Twin (DT) spans multiple phases, beginning with **conceptualization and design**, where abstract models (e.g., CAD representations of physical components like car chassis or wheel dimensions) are developed to capture static and geometric properties. This phase transitions into **dynamic modeling**, where engineers construct physics-based models (e.g., differential equations for force dynamics) to simulate behavior under operational conditions. Post-development, the DT integrates **real-time data acquisition** from the Physical Twin (PT) via sensors, enabling **calibration and parameter estimation** through algorithms that refine models (e.g., updating Cyber-Physical System (CPS) and environmental parameters). The lifecycle includes **monitoring and validation**, where the DT employs algorithms (e.g., fault detectors, state estimators) to verify system performance against operational constraints. **Optimization** is embedded as an iterative process, using data-driven insights to adjust PT configurations or operational policies. Transition management between phases relies on **model persistence**—retaining pre-deployment models in the DT to enable continuous comparison between simulated and actual PT behavior. Lifecycle governance adheres to **iterative refinement principles**, ensuring alignment between evolving PT requirements and DT fidelity, with explicit governance frameworks for model versioning, data provenance, and compliance with operational constraints (e.g., safety thresholds)."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system prioritizes **model fidelity** and **validity** through structured methodologies:
- **Fidelity requirements**: Models must accurately represent both the **cyber** (e.g., control systems) and **physical** (e.g., structural dynamics) aspects of the PT and its environment, ensuring predictive simulations align with real-world behavior.
- **Validation and verification methods**: Models are validated against empirical data from sensors, while verification involves cross-checking analytical formulations (e.g., differential equations) with observed system responses. **Calibration algorithms** adjust model parameters to minimize discrepancies between predicted and actual behavior.
- **Uncertainty sources and quantification**: Uncertainties arise from sensor noise, environmental variability, and model simplifications. These are quantified through statistical analysis of sensor data and model residuals, enabling probabilistic assessments of system performance.
- **Error detection and correction mechanisms**: Monitoring algorithms (e.g., fault detectors, state estimators) identify deviations from expected behavior, triggering corrective actions such as parameter recalibration or human intervention.
- **Quality assurance processes**: Continuous validation ensures models remain aligned with the PT’s evolving state. The DT framework enforces **closed-loop feedback** between data, models, and algorithms to sustain high fidelity, with human oversight for critical decisions (e.g., system adjustments during violations).
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin’s Technical Connection** relies on a **hybrid communication architecture** integrating **RabbitMQ** (an open-source message broker) and **1-Wire bus** protocols. RabbitMQ operates as the primary **asynchronous messaging middleware**, hosted on the Raspberry Pi, enabling decoupled communication between sensors (PT), controllers, and DT components (e.g., monitors, calibration algorithms). The 1-Wire bus facilitates **low-level sensor data aggregation**, allowing multiple DS18S20 temperature sensors to share a single communication line while maintaining individual addressability. Network topology is **star-topology-centric** (via RabbitMQ) with **point-to-point sensor connections** (1-Wire bus). Bandwidth and latency requirements are not explicitly quantified, but the system assumes **low-latency asynchronous messaging** (critical for real-time calibration and monitoring). Reliability mechanisms include **RabbitMQ’s persistent message queues** and **redundant sensor polling** (via the low-level driver). Security protocols are not detailed, though RabbitMQ supports **TLS/SSL encryption** (implied for sensitive data flows). The infrastructure ensures **end-to-end connectivity** between PT sensors, the Raspberry Pi-based controller, and DT algorithms via RabbitMQ’s publish-subscribe model.

Key protocols: **RabbitMQ (AMQP)**, **1-Wire bus (DS18S20)**.
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The **deployment infrastructure** for the Digital Twin system is not explicitly detailed in the provided document. The document does not specify **hosting platforms** (e.g., cloud, edge, on-premise), **computational/storage requirements**, or **scalability mechanisms**. No mention is made of **deployment models** (e.g., containerized, virtualized) or **availability/reliability assurance** (e.g., redundancy, failover). Thus, this characteristic is **Not in Document**."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin (DT) incorporates a **safe protocol** embedded within the low-level driver to ensure the Physical Twin (PT) remains in a **safe operational state** under all conditions. The system employs **monitoring algorithms** that continuously assess whether the PT is performing **safely and optimally** by comparing real-time data against calibrated models of the Cyber-Physical System (CPS) and its environment. When deviations or violations are detected, the system triggers **corrective actions**, which may be automated or manually intervened by human operators. The DT framework does not explicitly detail **cybersecurity protocols**, **access control mechanisms**, or **authentication systems**, but the **safety-critical nature** of the DT is emphasized through its reliance on **calibration algorithms** and **real-time monitoring** to prevent unsafe states. No explicit mention of **risk assessment approaches**, **fail-safe mechanisms**, or **threat mitigation strategies** is provided in the documents."
]
